subject: Physics
title: Is the Universe a Simulation?
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Is the Universe a Simulation?.

hook.text: Nick Bostrom argued that if civilizations can run ancestor simulations, we probably live inside one.

buildup.text: If a future civilization has enough computing power, it could simulate entire universes with conscious beings.

discovery.text: If many such simulations run, simulated beings vastly outnumber real ones. Statistically, we're likely simulated.

twist.text: Quantum mechanics' pixelated behavior and the speed of light as a processing limit feel suspiciously computational.

climax.text: Most physicists are skeptical, but the hypothesis is surprisingly hard to disprove. It's philosophy meets physics.

punchline.text: A logical argument with no way to test it. The ultimate open question.

quiz.question: What is the core of the simulation argument?
options: If civilizations can simulate universes, most conscious beings would exist in simulations
Computers will eventually become as smart as humans
The universe is a hologram projected from a 2D surface
